prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. Presidential elections were held in Egypt between 26 and 28 May 2014. There were only two candidates, former Egyptian defence minister Abdel Fattah el-Sisi and Egyptian Popular Current candidate Hamdeen Sabahi. El-Sisi won the election in a landslide victory, having received 97% of votes. Before announcing his candidacy in the election, el-Sisi, who as Defence Minister also served as Commander-in-Chief of the Egyptian Armed Forces, was responsible for officially announcing the removal of president Mohamed Morsi from office in the aftermath of the June 2013 Egyptian protests. After Morsi's removal, Sisi installed a temporary interim government, but remained Egypt's Minister of Defence and assumed the role of the country's First Deputy Prime Minister. On 26 March 2014, he officially retired from the military, and announced that he would
B. Between May 26 and May 28, 2014, Egypt conducted its presidential elections. The ballot featured only two contenders: Abdel Fattah el-Sisi, who previously served as Egypt's defense minister, and Hamdeen Sabahi, representing the Egyptian Popular Current. El-Sisi secured a decisive win in the election, garnering an overwhelming 97% of the total votes cast. Prior to declaring his intention to run for president, el-Sisi, in his capacity as both Defense Minister and Commander-in-Chief of the Egyptian Armed Forces, had publicly declared the removal of President Mohamed Morsi from power. This declaration followed the widespread Egyptian protests that occurred in June 2013. Subsequent to Morsi's ousting, Sisi established a provisional interim government. He concurrently retained his position as Egypt's Minister of Defense and took on the duties of the nation's First Deputy Prime Minister. On March 26, 2014, he formally ended his military career and subsequently revealed his intention to pursue the presidency.
C. Egypt held presidential elections from 26 to 28 May 2014, with only two candidates - former Egyptian defence minister Abdel Fattah el-Sisi and Egyptian Popular Current candidate Hamdeen Sabahi - in the running. El-Sisi emerged victorious with a landslide 97% of the vote, while Sisi, who held the position of Defence Minister and Commander-in-Chief of the Egyptian Armed Forces, officially announced the ousting of president Mohamed Morsi in June 2013 as the interim he remained in

Answer:
